name: NSAP - Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme

sections.details:
The "Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)" is one of the five sub-schemes of the National Social Assistance Programme, Ministry of Rural Development. Under this scheme, persons belonging to BPL households and 60 years or above in age are eligible to apply. This program is being implemented in rural areas as well as urban areas.
The Government of India, on 15th August 1995, introduced the National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP) as a fully funded Centrally Sponsored Scheme targeting the destitute, defined as any person who has little or no regular means of subsistence from his/her own source of income or through financial support from family members or other sources.
> **The NSAP at present includes five sub-schemes as its components-**

a) Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)
b) Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
c) Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS)
d) National Family Benefit Scheme (NFBS)
e) Annapurna Scheme
> **Objectives of NSAP-**

1. Provides social assistance benefits to poor households in the case of death, maternity, or old age of the breadwinner.
2. Ensure minimum national standards, in addition to the benefits that the states are currently providing or might provide in the future.
3. Ensure uniform social protection for the beneficiaries across the country without interruption.
> **Expansion to cover all eligible BPL persons-**

In 2007, the scheme was expanded to cover all eligible persons Below Poverty Line (BPL).
sections.benefits:
- ₹200 per month for beneficiaries aged 60–79 years.
- ₹500 per month for beneficiaries aged 80 years and above.

> **Note:** At present old age beneficiaries are getting anywhere between ₹200/- to ₹1000/- depending on the State Contribution.
sections.eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) household.
1. The applicant should be 60 years of age or above.
sections.application:
### Application mode: Online

**Step 01:** One can download UMANG App or visit the website [https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home](https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home)
**Step 02:** The citizen can log in using their mobile number and OTP.
**Step 03:** Once logged in, the citizen can search for NSAP.
**Step 04:** Click on “Apply Online”
**Step 05:** Fill in the basic details, choose the mode of payment of pension, upload photo and click on "Submit."
sections.documents:
- Passport-size Photo
- Domicile Certificate
- Residential Proof (Voter card/Electricity Bill/Aadhar Card)
- Age Proof (Birth Certificate issued by the School last attended or Municipal authority or SHO or through the medical board)
- Aadhaar Number
- Bank Passbook
- Ration Card
- Affidavit duly attested by Judicial Magistrate/Executive Magistrate that she/he is not in receipt of any pension/financial assistance from any other source
sections.faq:
**Q: What Does NSAP Stand For And When Was It Launched?**

A: NSAP stands for National Social Assistance Programme. NSAP was launched on 15th August 1995.

**Q: What Is The Objective Of NSAP?**

A: In providing social assistance benefits to poor households in the case of old age, death of the breadwinner, and maternity, the NSAP aims at ensuring minimum national standards, in addition to the benefits that the States are currently providing or might provide in future.

**Q: What Are The Components Of NSAP?**

A: Presently NSAP comprises of five schemes, namely - (1) Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS), (2) Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS), (3) Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme (IGNDPS), (4) National Family Benefit Scheme NFBS) and (5) Annapurna.

**Q: Who Implements NSAP?**

A: The NSAP is implemented in the States/UTs in accordance with the general conditions applicable to all components of the NSAP as well as specific conditions applicable to each component. The NSAP schemes are implemented by the Rural Development Department.

**Q: What Is The Basic Eligibility Criterion Under NSAP?**

A: For getting benefits under NSAP the applicant must belong to a Below Poverty Line (BPL) family according to the criteria prescribed by the Govt. of India.

**Q: How Are The Beneficiaries Identified In Rural Areas?**

A: As per the revised eligibility criteria, new beneficiaries will be identified from the BPL list prepared by the States/UTs as per guidelines issued by the Ministry of Rural Development (MORD) for the BPL Census 2002.

**Q: How Will The Identification Of The Beneficiaries Be Done In The Urban Areas?**

A: Identification of eligible beneficiaries will be carried out as per the BPL list required to be prepared in connection with the poverty alleviation program off the ministry of Urban Housing and Poverty Alleviation.

**Q: What Will Be The Position In Respect Of Existing Beneficiaries, Whose Names Are Not On The BPL List?**

A: They will continue to get the pension as at present without any interruption notwithstanding the fact that their names are not borne on the BPL list, provided they were eligible as per the old criteria.

**Q: What Is The Funding Pattern Envisaged Under NSAP?**

A: Under NSAP 100 percent Central Assistance is extended to the States/UTs to provide the benefits in accordance with the norms, guidelines, and conditions laid down by the Central Government.

**Q: What Is The Eligibility For Getting A Pension Under IGNOAPS?**

A: The age of the applicant should be 60 years or above and belong to a household living below the poverty line.

**Q: How Is IGNOAPS Different From The Earlier National Old Age Pension Scheme (NOAPS)?**

A: Old age pensions were granted to a person who is 65 years old or higher and who is destitute in the sense of having little or no means of regular income. Pension under IGNOAPS is now granted to a person who is 60 years or above and belongs to a household below the poverty line instead of only to the the destitute.

**Q: When Did The IGNOAPS Come Into Effect?**

A: The National Old Age Pension Scheme has been renamed as Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOPS) and was formally launched on 19th November 2007.

**Q: What Is The Pension Amount Under Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme? Whether Pension Is Restricted To Only One Person In A Family?**

A: ₹200/- per month per beneficiary up to 79 years and ₹500/- per month per beneficiary from 80 year onwards.
